description:
The <tt>/tmp</tt> directory is a world-writable directory used
for temporary file storage. Ensure it has its own partition or
logical volume at installation time, or migrate it using LVM.
rationale:
The <tt>/tmp</tt> partition is used as temporary storage by many programs.
Placing <tt>/tmp</tt> in its own partition enables the setting of more
restrictive mount options, which can help protect programs which use it.
ocil_clause: "/tmp is not a mountpoint" is returned
ocil:
Verify that a separate file system/partition has been created for /tmp with the following command: 
$ mountpoint /tmp
fixtext: Migrate the "/tmp" path onto a separate file system.
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must use a separate file system for /tmp.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must use a separate file system for /tmp.
stig.vuldiscussion: The "/tmp" partition is used as temporary storage by many programs. Placing "/tmp" in its own partition enables the setting of more restrictive mount options, which can help protect programs that use it.
stig.checktext:
Verify that a separate file system/partition has been created for "/tmp" with the following command:

$ mount | grep /tmp

/dev/mapper/rhel-tmp on /tmp type xfs (rw,nodev,nosuid,noexec,seclabel)

If a separate entry for "/tmp" is not in use, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext: Migrate the "/tmp" path onto a separate file system.

title: Ensure /tmp Located On Separate Partition
